1k magnetic structure
k-maximal magnetic symmetry (4 possible)
The model includes a relation 1/2 between the moments of the two independent Ni atoms. This restricts the configuration to a primary irrep mode (spin wave) mU2. A second irrep mode mA2 with k2 = (0 0 1/2) is symmetry-allowed (third harmonic), and its possible presence makes the two Ni sites symmetry-independent, its collinearity not being symmetry-forced. The presence of this secondary third harmonic is also necessary to have a model with equal moduli of all moments.

The high temperature phase with SG P-42_1m is taken here as parent phase for the representational analysis.
The irrep decomposition of the structure therefore includes non-magnetic irreps that are not listed here. The propagation vector given above refers to the spin arrangement. The presence of the structural modulation allows the presence of non-zero magnetic propagation vectors, as induced secondary harmonics: DT ((0,1/3,0),(1/3,0,0)) and SM ((1/3,1/3,0), (1/3,-1/3,0)). Their amplitudes are however negligible.
The refinement of a model with all symmetry-allowed spin parameters set free yielded deviations smaller than the standard deviations from the present simpler model, which is constrained to the compatible k=0 magnetic irrep (along a special direction). The role of this irrep can be considered primary when considered together with the structural modulation that reduces the SG to P2_12_12.

2 different irreps, with different propagation vectors, are active as primary ones, and their amplitude is equal.
Despite involving two different irreps, the transition from the AFQ phase is reported to be continuous. But these irreps and propagation vectors are defined with respect to the cubic phase of symmetry Pm-3m, as parent. and not with respect to the AFQ phase inmediate above in temperature.
Among the secondary irreps allowed by the magnetic ordering of the present model two irreps with wave vector R exist: R5+ and R4+. The irrep R5+ describes the transformation properties of the quadrupolar components (Qxy,Qxz,Qyz) and for the reported magnetic ordering it must be restricted to (Qxy,0,0), with a symmetry I4/mmm(a+b,-a+b,2c;1/2,1/2,0).
Hence, from this magnetic structure one can infer that the space group of the AFQ phase is I4/mmm, with its order parameter being the QP component Qxy (and domain-related equivalent components Qxz and Qyz). In this AFQ tetragonal phase the Ce site does not split but its primitive unit cell contains two sites, related by the 4-fold rotation and with opposite Qxy spontaneous values.
If the AFQ phase of symmetry I4/mmmm is taken as parent, the spin arrangement involves a single irrep of I4/mmmm, with only 2 propagation vectors active, namely: (1/2,0,0), (0,1/2,0), and from this viewpoint the structure can be considered a 2k-magnetic structure. Using this AFQ phase as parent, a single irrep is active, and it has two basis modes, i.e. two free parameters, which allow the spin arrangement to change the global orientation, while keeping the relative ortogonal directions of the spins.
The other two degrees of freedom for the spins within the MSG, correspond to a secondary irrep allowed by the magnetic symmetry.

The magnetic ordering of this model only reduces the parent symmetry into a monoclinc MSG, as the mM5- ordering is along a special direction. The monoclinic axis is along the (-1,1,0) direction of the parent structure. The unit cell strain of the reported triclinic unit cell with respect to the tetragonal one complies within its tolerances with monoclinic symmetry.
The reduction from monoclinic into a a triclinic symmetry is only produced by small displacements on the xy plane of the Ce1 and Ge1 atoms, from their (0,0,z) parent positions.
This small atomic displacements, which break further the symmetry, cannot be considered an induced effect of the magnetic ordering. It is either an artefact of the model or an additional symmetry-breaking structural distortion, i.e. a additional independent order parameter.

FM is symmetry-allowed and is observed.
Symmetry allows a FM perpendicular to the direction of the AFM componenton the basal ab plane. It has the same irrep properties as the AFM arrangement, and  it is coupled bilinearly with it, resulting in the observed weak FM.
Symmetry also allows an AFM component along c, corresponding to a different irrep coupled at a higher order with the mGM3+ configuration.
If the direction of the AFM mGM3 configuration is along a or equivalent the MSG is then C2'/c', with a secondary FM component corresponding to a secondary irrep also allowed along c (see entry # 0.112 for FeBO3).
By means of a magnetic field on the ab easy plane, which couples with the weak FM component, the orientation of the AFM arrangement can be controlled, changing the MSG from C2/c to C2'/c' (through C-1 for a general orientation).

The MSG allows a third irrep mGM5+ (AFM). This third irrep is present in the spin arrangement with a weight exactly of the same magnitude as that of mGM4+(FM) such that they yield the total moment of the atom Eu1_1 in the asymmetric unit, while together they nullify any contribution to the moments of the other four atoms, whose arrangement is only described by the irrep mX5-.  
The relative sign of the amplitudes of the mGM4+ and mGM5+ components in the spin arrangement with respect to that of the mX5- component is arbitrary, as they correspond to domain-equivalent configurations. This makes the relative sign of the moment of Eu1_1 irrelevant, both values yielding equivalent domains.

FM is symmetry-allowed and is observed.
symmetry only restricts the weak FM to be perpendicular to the direction of the AFM component, but the FM component having the same irrep properties as the AFM arrangement, and coupled bilinearly with it, necessarily lies on the basal ab plane. The additional allowed FM component along c corresponds to a different irrep coupled at a higher order.
If the direction of the AFM moments is along the (1,2,0) direction or equivalent the MSG is then C2/c, with the FM component constrained to the perpendicular a direction (see entry 0.113 for NiCO3).
By means of a magnetic field on the easy ab plane, which couples with the weak FM component, the orientation of the AFM arrangement can be controlled, changing the MSG from C2'/c' to C2/c (through C-1, for general orientations).

Symmetry allows a FM perpendicular to the direction of the AFM componenton the basal ab plane. It has the same irrep properties as the AFM arrangement, and  it is coupled bilinearly with it, resulting in the observed weak FM.
Symmetry also allows an AFM component along c, corresponding to a different irrep coupled at a higher order with the mGM3+ configuration.
If the direction of the AFM mGM3 configuration is along a or equivalent the MSG is then C2'/c', with a secondary FM component corresponding to a secondary irrep also allowed along c (see entry # 0.112 for FeBO3).
By means of a magnetic field on the ab easy plane, which couples with the weak FM component, the orientation of the AFM arrangement can be controlled, changing the MSG from C2/c to C2'/c' (through C-1 for a general orientation).

Two irreps of the hexagonal parent space group are involved as primary, corresponding to the two observed consecutive magnetic transitions.

The spin arrangement corresponds to a general direction within the irrep, with the lowest possible symmetry for this irrep.
The spin arrangement breaks the inversion center symmetry of the paramagnetic phase (the MSg is polar along (1,1,0)).
The arrangement of the larger Nd moments corresponds to a special direction of the active irrep yielding a higher symmetry P_abam, and the spin arrangement of the Nd atoms with much weaker moments also follow the P_abam symmetry, but according to a domain-related configuration. Both arrangements of higher equivalent symmetries but different groups, when superposed reduce the global MSG to the lowest possible one for the active irrep. A model with both sets of atoms consistently following the same ymmetry (by switching the weaker moment arrangement to the domain-related one by inversion) does not seem to have been tested in the refinement.

Very rare case, together with the other phase at lower temperature (see #0.174), where a strong magnetic splitting of a site is reported, with two atoms that are equivalent in the paramagnetic structure having very different spin moduli.
The reported symmetry breaking is reached through the presence of a spin mode according to a 2-dim irrep mGM5+ along a special direction, which accordingly must be taken as a primary order parameter. The MSG is not maximal and a secondary spin mode transforming according to the irrep mGM2+ and compatible with a higher hexagonal symmetry is also present in the spin arrangement. However, the amplitude of this second spin mode is much larger than the one that formally is the primary one.  Being rather fundamental in the spin arrangement. Therefore its role does not seem to be a mere secondary induced effect,

1k magnetic structure
Very rare case, together with the other phase at higher temperature (see #0.173), where a strong magnetic splitting of a site is reported, with two atoms that are equivalent in the paramagnetic phase having very different spin moduli.
The MSG of the structure allows the presence in the spin arrangement of spin modes corresponding to 4 different irreps, and all of them are present. Those with larger amplitudes are the mGM2+, mGM5+ and mGM6+. The amplitudes of the mGM2+ and mGM5+ spin modes are similar to their values in the preceding phase (see #0.173), the main difference being caused by the onset in this phase of two mGM6+ spin modes, which are sufficient to break the symmetry to the observed MSG.

The change of the magnetic ordering in this phase with respect to the magnetic phase above 220K (see #1.702) is due to a charge ordering structural transition resulting in a Pmma structural distortion.
The additional magnetic irrep mX1- active in this phase can be considered a secondary effect, induced by the primary structural distortion. For this reason we have included explicitly the irrep corresponding to this structural distortion as a primary irrep.
As the second magnetic propagation vector can be considered a secondary one with respect to the tetragonal parent phase, and there is only a single propagation vector, with respect to the Pmma distorted structure, we classify the structure as a 1k magnetic structure.
